ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Aug-16-2013 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:
Appearances: Michael Keck for Michael Countee.

The Court addressed the petition and inquired whether there had been any success in locating the other three heirs. Mr. Keck stated he had not located the other three heirs and that he has hired a private investigator to find them. The Court inquired whether Brandie Countee had located Davina Countee. Mr. Keck stated that Brandie Countee had not gotten back to him, that any additional expended funds should be taken out of the share of the three heirs that can't be located, and that if the heirs are not found then the funds will be turned over to the Treasurer. Mr. Keck noted that the heirs that have kept in touch should not be deprived their distribution. The matter was taken under submission.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Jacqueline Alameda. Bailiff: Deputy T. Bradley. (Not Reported)

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Jul-11-2013 Continued To Jul-30-13 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

The accounting petition was taken under submission.

Judge: John Dearman. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy T. Bradley.

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Jun-11-2013 Continued To Jul-02-13 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar May-28-2013 Continued To Jun-11-13 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Michael Keck for Michael Countee (Sucessor Administrator).

Mr. Keck explained his services preformed in regards to providing notices to the heirs and provided case background. The Court instructed Mr. Keck to file a declaration addressing the following issues: Mr. Toplen's receiving extraordinary fees, Mr. Keck receiving statutory fees, further attempts required in contacting the heirs, additional information as to why Anita and Michael should receive additional distribution, and the delinquency of not moving estate funds from an attorney-client trust account to an estate account. The Accounting Petition was continued to June 11, 2013 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Mary E. Wiss. Clerk: Nancy Bautista. Bailiff: Deputy Chien.

Status on the petition for final distribution was continued to February 13, 2013 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Michael Keck for Michael Countee.

Mr. Keck stated that the petition for final distribution is prepared, that the funds are in Mr. Tolpen's account, that Mr. Tolpen was supposed to transfer the funds to him and that he needs to obtain a Referee to value the property. Status on close of the Estate was continued to January 2, 2013 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: Peter Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy M. Benton.

Status on the Petition to Close Estate was continued to September 24, 2012 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status On Morgan Countee'S Accounting
Off Calendar Jun-25-2012 Off Calendar
MINI MINUTES: No appearance was made by any Party, Counsel or Interested Person.

This matter came on calendar for status on Ms. Countee's (Former Administrator) account. The Court stated that there has been no follow up on the matter for one year or more, and that the Estate still needs to be closed and that it is the responsibility of the current Administrator to do so. Status on Ms. Countee's account was taken off calendar. Status on a petition for final distribution or approval of a status report was set for August 27, 2012 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. The Probate Examiner is to inform Counsel for the current Administrator.

Judge: Peter Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy M. Loos.

Petition Fo Status Report Of Status Of Administration, For Issuance Of Citation To Compel Account And For Removal Of Administrator.
Off Calendar Dec-12-2007 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Farley Tolpen for the Michael Countee (Petitioner).

The Court stated that there was no order for the removal of Morgan Countee. Mr. Tolpen explained that a citation had not been served, therefore the order had not been signed. In addition, Mr. Tolpen stated that the citation had been served. The Court questioned bond in regards to the appointment of Michael Countee. Mr. Tolpen stated that the house was the only asset, which was sold by the former administrator, and that if the assets were recovered, Michael Countee would post bond. The matter was taken under submission.

Judge: John Dearman. Reporter: Katy Bersamin CSR#11722. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Felicia Smith.

Notice was mailed to the house but they have not been able to serve the Citation. Counsel will ask for a new Citation. Court instructed counsel to file a petition for appointment of successor administrator. Matter is continued to 12/12. The petition for appointment of successor administrator can be set on the same date.
